pages.text:
Statement of Faith and Theological Convictions
About Pastor Daniel Folarin
Pastor Daniel Folarin is a Christ-centered pastor , teacher , author , and theologian committed to helping
people see the beauty of Jesus Christ and experience the transforming power of union with Him.
His ministry is rooted in the conviction that the gospel is not merely about escaping judgment, but about
participating in the life of God through Christ. He believes that the Christian life is the life of Christ
expressed in and through believers by the Holy Spirit.
The Authority of Scripture
We believe that the Holy Scriptures are inspired by God and are the supreme authority for faith, doctrine,
and Christian living. The Bible reveals God's redemptive purpose in Christ and finds its fulfillment in Jesus.
Scripture is best understood through a Christ-centered lens, recognizing that all of God's promises find their
fulfillment in Him.
The Triune God
We believe in one God, eternally existing as Father , Son, and Holy Spirit.
The Father is the source of all things. The Son is the eternal Word made flesh, the perfect revelation of God.
The Holy Spirit indwells, empowers, transforms, and leads believers into the life of Christ.
Jesus Christ
We believe Jesus Christ is fully God and fully man.
Through His incarnation, life, death, resurrection, ascension, and present reign, He has accomplished the
complete work of redemption.
Jesus is the center of all Scripture, the image of the invisible God, and the true revelation of humanity as
God intended it to be.
Salvation
We believe salvation is a gift of God's grace received through faith in Jesus Christ.
Through Christ's finished work, believers are forgiven, justified, reconciled to God, and adopted into His
family.
1

Salvation is not earned by human effort but received through faith in the gospel.
We believe the believer's security rests in the faithfulness of Christ and not in human performance.
Union With Christ
We believe the central reality of the Christian life is union with Christ.
Through the Holy Spirit, believers participate in the life of Christ and are called to grow into the fullness of
His likeness.
The Christian life is not merely imitation of Christ but participation in Christ.
The Holy Spirit
We believe the Holy Spirit dwells within every believer and empowers them for holy living, spiritual growth,
service, and witness.
The Holy Spirit illuminates Scripture, produces the fruit of Christlike character , distributes spiritual gifts, and
leads believers into maturity.
We affirm the present-day ministry and operation of the gifts of the Spirit.
The Church
We believe the Church is the body of Christ and the family of God on earth.
The Church exists to worship God, make disciples, proclaim the gospel, care for one another , and participate
in God's mission in the world.
Every believer is called to meaningful participation in the life of the local church.
Spiritual Formation and Discipleship
We believe that discipleship is the lifelong process of being transformed into the image of Jesus Christ.
This transformation occurs through Scripture, prayer , worship, fellowship, service, obedience, and the
ongoing work of the Holy Spirit.
The goal of discipleship is Christlikeness.
Prayer
We believe prayer is communion with God and participation in His purposes.
2

Prayer is not merely a religious duty but a relationship of trust, dependence, worship, and partnership with
God.
The Kingdom of God
We believe the Kingdom of God has been inaugurated through the life, death, resurrection, and reign of
Jesus Christ.
Believers are called to live as citizens of God's Kingdom, demonstrating the character , justice, mercy, and
love of Christ in the world.
New Creation
We believe that through Christ, God is making all things new.
The resurrection of Jesus is the beginning of God's new creation, and believers are invited to participate in
that new creation life here and now.
Eschatology
We believe Jesus Christ reigns as Lord and King.
We approach biblical prophecy through a Christ-centered and victorious framework, emphasizing the
triumph of Christ, the advancement of His Kingdom, and the hope of the new creation.
Our focus is not fear but faith, not speculation but discipleship, and not escapism but faithful participation
in God's mission.
Mission
We believe every believer is called to participate in God's mission.
The gospel is good news for every person, culture, and nation.
The Church exists not merely to gather but to go, making disciples and embodying the love of Christ in the
world.
Our Desire
Our desire is to help people know Christ deeply, live from their identity in Him, grow in spiritual maturity,
participate in the life of the Spirit, and become faithful witnesses of God's Kingdom for the glory of God and
the good of the world.
3
